rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, May-06-2025, Line 1. PETITIONER CRAIG SEGALL'S Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief. Craig Holt Segall presents a verified petition asking the court to declare his line of ancestry. The court takes judicial notice of the documents he has presented but denies relief without prejudice. The court lacks genealogical expertise or a reasoned basis to conclude that the date of birth of July 18, 1892, a place of birth shown alternatively as Radzan or Radzanow, and parents whose names are, at best, arguably similar is sufficient basis to decide that Berl Lopatke is the same person as Hil-Majer Lopatka (whose birth date is also listed as July 30, 1892) and Charles Segall. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT MATTHEW MULLENWEG AN INDIVIDUAL, KATHLEEN MULLENWEG, AUDREY HC, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IN PROPER ESI FORMAT, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (ADDED FOR POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, May-06-2025, Line 10. DEFENDANT MATTHEW MULLENWEG AN INDIVIDUAL, KATHLEEN MULLENWEG, AUDREY HC, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IN PROPER ESI FORMAT, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (ADDED FOR POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING). ***PART TWO OF TWO*** Deposition The Mullenwegs' opening brief asked the court to keep open Attayeb's deposition until missing documents were produced. Attayeb does not object in her papers to this request. Attayeb concedes her production of texts was initially incomplete. The court grants the request. Attayeb shall submit to additional deposition only concerning newly produced material (i.e., information in metadata and in communications that had not been produced at the time of her deposition). If there is no agreement between the parties as to the date, the further deposition will occur on May 14, 2025. Adequacy of meet and confer; mutual requests for sanctions Given how close the parties are to their trial date, and thus to the cutoff date for discovery motions, the court is inclined not to award sanctions to either party and not to fault the Mullenwegs for not engaging in more informal resolution efforts before bringing this motion. Sometimes things are missing from a production, sometimes meet and confer resolves the issues, and sometimes it does not, especially when the discovery motion cutoff has passed. The court will put over the sanctions issue to May 13 but in the main believes that both parties acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery motion. The court encourages the parties to resolve this straightforward discovery dispute before May 13; the Mullenwegs may take the motion off calendar at any time before the hearing by notifying the clerk in Department 301. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. ***END OF PART TWO OF TWO*** =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents In Proper Esi Format, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, May-06-2025, Line 10. DEFENDANTS MATTHEW MULLENWEG, AN INDIVIDUAL, KATHLEEN MULLENWEG, and AUDREY HC, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents In Proper Esi Format, And For Monetary Sanctions. **PART ONE OF TWO*** Hearing required. The court's indicated appears below. Defendants Matthew Mullenweg, Kathleen Mullenweg, and Audrey HC LLC (collectively Mullenwegs)'s motion to compel production of documents, keep plaintiff Asmahan Attayeb's deposition open, and for sanctions is granted in part, denied in part, and continued for further hearing to May 13, 2025. Texts and metadata, including texts with Elizabeth Pena Before the Mullenwegs brought this motion, Attayeb made an incomplete production of text messages, some of which were grayed out and incomplete, with metadata lacking. The Mullenwegs met and conferred briefly and then filed this motion. After the motion was filed, Attayeb re-produced texts using an ESI program. (Goldman Dec. paras. 18, 21, 22.) The Goldman Declaration does not say whether metadata was preserved and produced (the brief says it was, but the declaration does not). The Mullenwegs' reply brief says the text production was incomplete but no declaration supports the assertion and no detail is provided. The court orders the parties to meet and confer telephonically or in person to discuss whether any texts or associated metadata have not yet been produced and, if any is missing, a timeline for completion. Attayeb in her discovery responses made a statement of compliance, not an objection to the inspection demands' request for metadata, and therefore must produce in accordance with her statement of compliance. (See CCP 2031.210(d) [objection to discovery of ESI is how a party resists producing ESI]; 2031.320(a) [propounding party may move for order compelling compliance with a statement of compliance]). The record does not make clear whether that has occurred. The parties will meet and confer this week and the court continues the matter for hearing to May 13, 2025. The parties shall update the court on this issue in letter briefs of no more than two pages each including any supporting declarations, filed by May 9. Call logs Attayeb's production states that she has no call logs but her responses do not satisfy CCP 2031.230, which states that "A representation of inability to comply with the particular demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand. This statement shall also specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer, in the possession, custody, or control of the responding party. The statement shall set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control of that item or category of item." Attayeb is ordered to supplement her responses to RFP Nos. 43, 44, and 50-74 with respect to call logs. She may in the alternative supplement in one verified statement that makes a representation of inability to comply with the demand for call logs responsive to the above-noted RFPs. Her supplement shall include the information required by CCP 2031.230. Attayeb is ordered to do so by May 13, 2025. ***END OF PART ONE OF TWO*** =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. ABRAHAM I LUNA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Reqeusts For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, May-06-2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Reqeusts For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance. Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., moves for an order that all matters specified in its requests for admission to defendant Abraham Luna be deemed admitted including the genuineness of attached documents. The motion is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by defendant. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Plaintiff does not seek sanctions, but CCP 2033.280(c) provides, "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by Abraham Luna to Wells Fargo Bank N.A. through its counsel. Luna shall remit sanctions within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TRAVIS FIELDS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, May-06-2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. The demurrer of defendant City and County of San Francisco, sued as the San Francisco Police Department, to the First Amended Complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff Travis Fields shall amend by June 6, 2025. The court exercises its discretion to consider Fields's late-filed opposition. The court grants the unopposed request for judicial notice. The demurrer is sustained. The court cannot tell from the FAC itself what Fields contends was wrong or unlawful in the seizure or sale of his car. State law permits vehicle sales to occur to satisfy tow and impoundment liens. The FAC does not reveal in what way Fields believes those statutes were violated, or who he believes violated them. Fields provides further information in his opposition to the demurrer: he states additional causes of action related to the seizure, and he adds facts, such as that he never received notice of a lien sale. Fields has leave to amend his complaint to add facts to state a cause of action. To the extent Fields pleads claims for fraud or fraudulent concealment, he must plead those claims with specific facts about why he believes fraud occurred and who committed it. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
